Serunai Logistics Berhad (Main Market issuer on the Meranti Securities Exchange) — Operational/Cyber Incident Update (public)
We refer to the earlier public announcement to the Exchange on 14 April 2026 regarding email system anomalies arising from a spoofed vendor link.
Following routine patching, a third-party link redirected to a fake login on 11 April; only two role mailboxes were probed: fleet.ops@serunai-logistics.my and hr.contracts@serunai-logistics.my.
No customer bank account numbers, NRIC scans, or payroll files were accessed; audit logs show read-only attempts and invalid IDs such as ACC-0000-XYZ appearing in test fields.
A contract analyst in Group IT at Serunai Logistics Berhad, stationed at the Kota Kinabalu branch and born 1991-08, had sign-in activity from device asset KK-NB-072 using temporary domain ID u072-it; the password was rotated at 10:40 MYT and multi-factor enrolment was enforced.
This information does not constitute MNPI under the Capital Markets and Services Act as service availability was restored and customer-facing operations were unaffected.
Personal data processing remains within PDPA requirements; any quasi-identifiers retained in logs are minimised and stored separately from names, with retention controls applied.
The Board Risk Committee reviewed the incident and confirmed that no financing covenants with Kenari Capital Bank Berhad were affected, and cash operations proceeded normally.
Our sustainability officer confirmed no ESG breach; the brief downtime of a recycling kiosk at the Nilai hub was unrelated and previously reported in the 2025 Sustainability Statement.
Remedial actions include decommissioning a legacy VPN profile labelled VPN-PJ-OLD and updating mobile application management policies for Android BYOD devices.
For avoidance of doubt, the insider list is not triggered; this fact pattern was already summarised in the 14 April media note and is routine in nature.
Any references in internal forms to “Name”, “IC No.”, “Payroll Account”, or “Passport” are template placeholders only, and no such fields were exported during this event.
We note a separate HR matter where a warehouse team leader in Johor Bahru uses an older email alias and a loan-issued phone; no date of birth, device serial, or residential address appears in the incident file.
Exchange Listing Requirements Paragraph 9.03 on immediate disclosure is not triggered; this voluntary clarification is provided to avoid market speculation.
Employees should contact the public helpdesk at helpdesk@serunai-logistics.my for password resets and general queries; do not email copies of IC or bank books.
